title: ranunculus serpens subsp. polyanthemoides
intro: Ranunculus serpens subsp. polyanthemoides, also known as the creeping buttercup, is a perennial plant species belonging to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Ranunculus serpens subsp. polyanthemoides is often used in teas, infusions, and topical creams, although its use should be cautioned due to potential toxicity.
